BSK & Associates                                                    Geotechnical and Environmental Services

FAX Transmission

From:        Chris Chalfant                              Date:
To:          John Wallace                               Time:
Company:     Fair Political Practices - State of CA     FAX #:    916-327-2026
                                                        # pgs.:   13

John,

Thank-you for the copy of the Chalmers letter you sent. I am faxing you a
copy of the Waste Discharge Requirements for the Mendota Landfill and parts
of the request for proposals issued by Fresno County.

I would also like to note the following:

I was not a designated employee of the Regional Water Quality Control Board.
I did not, and was not, required to complete an annual statement of economic
interest.

Please call me if you need any more information.

Thank-you

Chris Chalfant




                                                                    9Z509

Our FAX number is 209-485-7427

1645 'E' Street, Suite 105  Fresno, CA  93706    (800) 669-3201 or (209) 445-3200

III.     SERVICES REQUIRED OF THE CONSULTANT

        Services of the consultant shall include, but not necessarily be
        limited to, the following:

        A.  Review Disposal Site files for required information.

        B.  Prepare timeline for project completion.

        C.  Prepare draft closure studies (summarized under Tasks a., b.,
            c., d., e., f., g., and h.; see Attachment "A") in accordance with
            Article 5, Chapter 15, title 23, California Code of Regulations
            and Title 14, California Code of Regulations, and submit to
            Fresno County for its review and approval. As stated, Fresno
            County will prepare the documentation required under Task e.

        D.  Prepare final closure studies and submit to the CRWQCB. Obtain
            approval of the required closure studies from the CRWQCB.

        E.  Attend meetings with Fresno County staff and the CWRQCB.


IV.     SERVICES PROVIDED BY FRESNO COUNTY

        A.  Provide access to Disposal Site files and assist with the
            location of Disposal Site information.

        B.  Review and approve draft closure studies prior to submittal of
            final documents to the CRWQCB.

        C.  Monitor progress of project to assure project deadlines are
            met.


V.      PROPOSAL SUBMITTAL REQUIREMENTS

        The submittal will be in two parts which are described in the
        following section:

        A.  The first part will enable Fresno County to assess the general
            qualifications of the consultant. This will involve the
            consultant answering the following questions in the sequence
            listed below:

            1. Firm name, address, and phone number.

            2. Type of organization (individual, partnership, or
               corporation).

            3. Firm principals who will be responsible for the project and
               their education, credentials, and experience.

            4. Key personnel who will work on the project with their
               education, credentials, and experience on comparable
               projects. Provide organizational chart of firm.


                                    -2-

20'd       L2HlS9P60Z 'ON X'd        HO1lO39/NHMGH 9OOS4S0dS9        29:9 O3M Z6-PL-LOO

Attachment "A"

CALIFORNIA REGIONAL WATER QUALITY CONTROL BOARD
CENTRAL VALLEY REGION

ORDER NO. 91-225

WASTE DISCHARGE REQUIREMENTS
FOR
COUNTY OF FRESNO
FOR
CLOSURE OF
MENDOTA SOLID WASTE DISPOSAL SITE
CLASS III LANDFILL
FRESNO COUNTY

The California Regional Water Quality Control Board, Central Valley Region,
(hereafter Board) finds that:

1. The County of Fresno, hereafter referred to as Discharger, owns an inactive
Class III landfill on the south bank of the Fresno Slough, approximately 1.5
miles northeast of the City of Mendota in Section 29, T13S, R15E, MD8&M, as
shown in Attachment A, which is incorporated herein as part of this Order.
The Assessor parcel numbers are 013-050-29T and 013-050-46ST.

2. The landfill was operated by the Discharger from 16 November 1969 to the
early 1980's. The facility was converted to a transfer station that
operated until 16 March 1982.

3. The Board, on 23 November 1970, adopted Resolution No. 71-144 which
prescribes requirements for the operation of an active waste management unit
(WMU). This Resolution no longer reflects the status of the WMU or current
regulations.

4. In 1987, the Discharger requested approval for use of dredged San Joaquin
River sediments as final cover. On 16 June 1987 approval was granted. The
Discharger has yet to apply this material. The WMU is currently covered by
approximately 0.5 feet of native soil.

5. The Discharger completed a Solid Waste Water Quality Assessment Test
investigation, pursuant to Section 13273 of the California Water Code, on
14 June 1988. The investigation detected trace concentrations of organic
contaminants in ground water. The investigation also indicated a possible
increase in minerals in ground water downgradient from the site.

DESCRIPTION OF THE SITE

6. The 21.9-acre site, as shown in Attachment A, contains an inactive landfill
WMU of approximately 20 acres. The boundaries of the WMU have yet to be
defined. Final grade of the WMU is approximately 160 feet USGS datum. The
inactive WMU may contain as much as 300,000 cubic yards of compacted
municipal and inert solid wastes.

CO'd                    L8bL/S9P60Z :ON XHJ         HOJIOG9/HINHH 00SS*RXS8         £S:6 ME 6Z-PI-L00

WASTE DISCHARGE REQUIREMENTS
COUNTY OF FRESNO FOR CLOSURE OF
MENDOTA SOLID WASTE DISPOSAL SITE
CLASS III LANDFILL
FRESNO COUNTY

-2-

7. Ground water elevations, measured in May of 1990, were 15 to 20 feet below
grade (145 to 140 feet USGS datum). Historic ground water levels have been
recorded as high as 7 to 9 feet below grade. Wastes are reported to have
been discharged up to 10 feet below grade (150 feet USGS datum). Ground
water has apparently penetrated 1 to 3 feet into the WMU.

8. Title 23, CCR, Section 2510 et seq. (Chapter 15) states in Section 2530(c)
that existing Class III WMUs shall be operated to ensure that wastes will
be a minimum 5 feet above the highest anticipated elevation of underlying
ground water.

9. The facility is adjacent to the Fresno Slough, which borders the north and
east sides of the WMU. The City of Mendota wastewater treatment plant
disposal ponds are on the west side of the WMU. Agricultural fields are to
the south of the WMU.

10. To the south and north of the WMU are several large capacity agricultural
wells. Shallow ground water is discharged to the Fresno Slough upstream from
about 1300 gpm. The water is discharged to the Fresno Slough upstream from
the WMU. The discharge replaces water withdrawn by Westlands Water District
downstream from the site. The pumping ground water probably removing ground water
impacted by site operations and diluting it prior to discharge.

11. Ground water quality is currently monitored at the WMU by a network of 5
monitoring wells on the west and south sides of the unit. Due to the
proximity of the Fresno Slough and wastewater disposal ponds there is no
background monitoring well. Area ground water, as typified by a domestic
well located 0.5 miles northwest of the WMU, is considered good with an
electrical conductivity of about 800 micromhos/cm. Surface water quality
is also considered good with an electrical conductivity of about 800
micromhos/cm.

12. The beneficial uses of ground water are domestic, municipal, agricultural,
and industrial supply.

13. Native soils in the area consist of Quaternary flood deposits made up of a
heterogenous mixture of sands, silts, and clays.

14. Land within 1,000 feet of the facility is used for agriculture, wastewater
treatment, and recreation.

15. No fault hazard zones are in the area of the facility.

16. The facility receives an average of 7 inches of precipitation per year.
Mean evaporation is approximately 65 inches per year.

WASTE DISCHARGE REQUIREMENTS
COUNTY OF FRESNO FOR CLOSURE OF
MENDOTA SOLID WASTE DISPOSAL SITE
CLASS III LANDFILL
FRESNO COUNTY

-3-

17. The 100-year, 24-hour precipitation event for the facility is 1.75 inches,
as calculated by Pearson type III distribution.

18. The facility is subject to shallow (2 to 3 feet) flooding during a 100-year
flood as identified on Federal Flood Insurance Map 065029 0820B (1 December
1982).

19. Surface drainage from the topography is toward the Fresno Slough, a valley
floor water and tributary to the San Joaquin River. The facility is in the
South Valley Floor Hydrological Area (No. 555.70).

20. The beneficial uses of Valley Floor Waters are domestic, municipal,
agricultural, and industrial supply; ground water recharge; power
generation; recreation; aesthetic enjoyment; navigation; fresh water
replenishment; and preservation and enhancement of fish, wildlife, and other
aquatic resources.

CEQA CONSIDERATIONS

21. The action to update waste discharge requirements for this facility is
exempt from the provisions of the California Environmental Quality Act
(Public Resources Code, Section 21000, et seq.), in accordance with 14 CCR,
Section 15301.

OTHER

22. The Water Quality Control Plan for the Tulare Lake Basin (5D) was adopted
on 21 August 1975. This Order implements that Plan.

23. All local agencies with jurisdiction to regulate land use, solid waste
disposal, and air pollution, and to protect public health, have approved the
use of this site for the discharges of waste stated herein.

24. The Board has notified the Discharger and interested agencies and persons
of its intent to prescribe waste discharge requirements for this discharge,
and provided them with an opportunity for a public hearing and an
opportunity to submit their written views and recommendations.

25. In a public hearing, the Board heard and considered all comments pertaining
to this facility and discharge.

IT IS HEREBY ORDERED that Resolution No. 71-144 be rescinded and that Fresno
County, its agents, successors, and assigns, in order to meet the provisions of
Division 7 of the California Water Code and the regulations adopted thereunder,
shall comply with the following at the inactive Mendota landfill.

90·d                    LZHLSGP60Z 'ON XHd        HOHHOCH/NIHOH OOSSHOOS    ts·ts   OdH Zb-PI-OOL

WASTE DISCHARGE REQUIREMENTS
COUNTY OF FRESNO FOR CLOSURE OF
MENDOTA SOLID WASTE DISPOSAL SITE
CLASS III LANDFILL
FRESNO COUNTY


A. Prohibitions:

   1. Disposal of wastes at this facility is prohibited.

B. Discharge Specifications:

                              GENERAL SPECIFICATIONS

   1. Waste shall be confined to the WMU described in Finding No. 6. and
      identified by the survey described in Provision C.14(h).

   2. A minimum 5 foot separation shall be maintained between landfilled wastes
      and the highest anticipated elevation of ground water.

   3. Water used for facility maintenance shall be limited to the minimum
      amount necessary for dust control and essential cover irrigation.

                        WATER QUALITY PROTECTION STANDARDS

   4. The concentrations of monitoring parameters or constituents of concern
      in waters passing through Points of Compliance shall not exceed the
      in waters passing through the Points of Compliance shall not exceed the
      concentration limit included in the water quality protection standard for
      groundwater, surface water, and vadose zone waters established pursuant
      to Provision C. 14(c) and incorporated in Monitoring and Reporting
      Program No. 91-225.

                          PROTECTION FROM STORM EVENTS

   5. The Disposal area shall be protected from any washout or erosion of waste
      or covering material, and from inundation, which could occur as a result
      of floods having a predicted frequency of once in 100 years.

   6. Surface drainage from tributary areas and internal site drainage from
      surface or subsurface sources shall not contact or percolate through
      wastes.

   7. Annually, prior to the anticipated rainy season, but no later than
      31 October, any necessary erosion control measures shall be implemented,
      and any necessary construction, maintenance, or repairs of precipitation
      and drainage control facilities shall be completed to prevent erosion or
      flooding of the facility and to prevent surface drainage from contacting
      or percolating through wastes.




90'd          L2b199P60Z 'ON XHJ         HO3IOBVOHIDIH HOSS*3XSSY       69:8 O3W Z6-H-10O

WASTE DISCHARGE REQUIREMENTS
COUNTY OF FRESNO FOR CLOSURE OF
MENDOTA SOLID WASTE DISPOSAL SITE
CLASS III LANDFILL
FRESNO COUNTY


8. Methane and other landfill gases shall be adequately vented, removed from
the WMU, or otherwise controlled to prevent the danger of explosion,
adverse health effects, nuisance conditions, or the impairment of
beneficial uses of water due to migration through the vadose
(unsaturated) zone.

WMU Closure Specifications

9. The WMU shall be closed in accordance with Chapter 15. The closure of
the WMU shall be under the direct supervision of a California registered
civil engineer or certified engineering geologist.

10. The closed WMU shall be provided with at least two permanent monuments,
installed by a licensed land surveyor or registered civil engineer, from
which the location and elevation of all wastes, containment structures,
and monitoring facilities can be determined throughout the post-closure
maintenance period.

11. At closure, the WMU shall receive a final cover which is designed and
constructed to function with minimum maintenance and consists, at a
minimum, of a 2-foot thick foundation layer which may contain waste
materials, overlain by a 1-foot thick, low permeability (1 X 10⁻⁷ cm/sec
or less) clay liner, and, finally, by a soil layer containing no
or leachate of not less than 1-foot thickness; or an engineered
equivalent final cover approved by the Executive Officer pursuant to
Subsections 2510(b) and (c) of Chapter 15.

12. Vegetation shall be planted and maintained over the closed WMU.
Vegetation shall be selected to require a minimum of irrigation and
maintenance and shall have a rooting depth less than the thickness of the
uppermost soil layer described in B.11.

13. The closed WMU shall be graded to at least a three percent (3%) grade and
maintained to prevent ponding.

14. Areas with slopes greater than ten percent, surface drainage courses, and
areas subject to erosion by wind or water shall be designed and
constructed to prevent such erosion.

C. Provisions:

1. The Discharger shall, in a timely manner, remove and relocate any wastes
discharged at this facility in violation of this Order.


LO'd                LZHLSGP602 ON XEd        HOTLOEHO/NIWDIH 0OSSHXOSS        66:6 MEd ZE-PI-1OO

WASTE DISCHARGE REQUIREMENTS                                    -6-
COUNTY OF FRESNO FOR CLOSURE OF
MENDOTA SOLID WASTE DISPOSAL SITE
CLASS III LANDFILL
FRESNO COUNTY


2.  The Discharger shall maintain a copy of this Order and make it available
     at all times to key personnel, who shall be familiar with its contents,
     and to regulatory agency personnel upon request.

3.  The Discharger shall notify the Board in writing of any proposed change
     in ownership or responsibility for maintenance of the facility. This
     notification shall be given 90-days prior to the effective date of the
     change and shall be accompanied by an amended Report of Waste Discharge
     and any technical documents that are needed to demonstrate continued
     compliance with this Order.

4.  The Discharger shall comply with all notice and reporting requirements
     of .he State Department of Water Resources with regard to the
     construction, alteration, destruction, or abandonment of all monitoring
     wells used for compliance with this Order, as required by Sections 13750
     through 13755 of the California Water Code.

5.  The Discharger shall comply with Monitoring and Reporting Program No. 91-
     225, which is attached to and made part of this Order.

6.  The Discharger shall immediately notify the Board of any flooding,
     equipment failure, slope failure, or other change in site conditions
     which could impair the integrity of waste or precipitation and drainage
     control structures.

                    WATER QUALITY PROTECTION STANDARD

7.  The Discharger shall submit a report pursuant to the time schedule
     outlined in Provision C.14(c), proposing a water quality protection
     standard for each media monitored (groundwater, surface water, and vadose
     zone).  The water quality protection standard shall include the
     following:

     a.  Constituents of concern, including all waste constituents, reaction
         products, and hazardous constituents that are reasonably expected to
         be in or derived from waste contained in the WMU, pursuant to
         Section 2550.3 of Chapter 15.

     b.  A medium specific concentration limit for each constituent of
         concern pursuant to Section 2550.4 of Chapter 15.

     c.  A scaled drawing of the WMU delineating the point of compliance at
         which the water quality protection standard will apply, pursuant to
         Section 2550.5 of Chapter 15. The point of compliance shall be a
         vertical surface located at the hydraulic downgradient limit of the
         WMU, extending through the uppermost aquifer underlying the unit.


80'd                    L2HL9€P60Z 'ON XVHX           HOHCOHH:HHdWH OOS5HXOSS           6€:6 OHM 6Z-H1-OL
00-T-H1-Z6 OHM €€:6

WASTE DISCHARGE REQUIREMENTS
COUNTY OF FRESNO FOR CLOSURE OF
MENDOTA SOLID WASTE DISPOSAL SITE
CLASS III LANDFILL
FRESNO COUNTY


d. A scaled drawing of the WMU delineating monitoring points at the
points of compliance and locations as required for the determination
of background water quality, pursuant to Section 2550.5 of Chapter
15. Monitoring points shall be sufficient in number, location, and
depth to provide the earliest possible detection of a release from
the WMU and adequately represent the quality of waters passing the
points of compliance. Groundwater monitoring points shall include
the zone(s) of highest hydraulic conductivity.

e. A compliance period pursuant to Section 2550.5 of Chapter 15.


WATER QUALITY MONITORING

8. Pursuant to the time schedule outlined in Provision C.14(b), the
Discharger shall propose for review and approval by the Executive Officer
and subsequent incorporation into Monitoring and Reporting Program No.
91-225, a detection monitoring program. The detection monitoring program
proposal shall include the following:

a. Detection monitoring programs for groundwater, surface water, and
the vadose zone pursuant to Section 2550.7(b)1 of Chapter 15.

b. A sampling plan including;

1. Proposed monitoring parameters and constituents of concern and
associated monitoring parameters pursuant to Section 2550.3
and 2550.8(e) of Chapter 15.

2. Proposed sampling methods pursuant to Section 2550.7(e)12 of
Chapter 15.

3. Proposed sampling frequency for monitoring parameters and
constituents of concern.

4. Proposed statistical methods for analysis of detection
monitoring data pursuant to Section 2550.7(e)7 and 2550.7(e)9
of Chapter 15.

c. Procedures for determination of background value for each
constituent of concern and monitoring parameter in each media
monitored, pursuant to Section 2550.7(e)10 of Chapter 15.

d. General water quality monitoring and systems requirements pursuant
to 2550.7 of Chapter 15.


60'd                L2HL98PO0Z 'ON XHJ        HO11O39/NI4d0 uOSSHOSS0        LS:E UEW 8E-PI-1OO

WASTE DISCHARGE REQUIREMENTS
COUNTY OF FRESNO FOR CLOSURE OF
MENDOTA SOLID WASTE DISPOSAL SITE
CLASS III LANDFILL
FRESNO COUNTY

-8-

e. A time schedule for implementation of a detection monitoring
program, including the start of any necessary construction within 30
days of Executive Officer approval of the proposed monitoring
program.

9. If the Discharger or the Board, through a detection monitoring program,
find that there is a statistical significant increase in monitoring
parameters or constituents of concern over the Water Quality Protection
Standard at or beyond the Point(s) of Compliance or, if the Discharger
or the Board determine there is significant physical evidence of a
release, the Discharger shall perform the following:

a. Immediately notif. the Board verbally of its findings and provide
written notification within seven days.

b. Immediately sample all monitoring points in the affected medium and
determine the concentration of all Constituents of Concern.

c. Notify the Board within 7 days if it intends to show that the
increase was due to either A) an error in sampling analysis, or
statistical evaluation, B) natural variation in the affected medium,
or C) some source other than the impoundments. Within 90 days of
this notification, the Discharger shall submit a report
demonstrating that the impoundments were not the cause of the
statistically significant increase. With that report, the
Discharger shall describe any appropriate changes in the detection
monitoring program.

d. Submit within 90 days, a report for the establishment of an
evaluation monitoring program as set forth in Sections 2550.9 and
2550.7(b)(1)(C) of Chapter 15. The report shall include;

1. The maximum concentration of each constituent of concern at
each monitoring point as determined by the most recent
sampling event.

2. Any proposed changes to the water quality monitoring system at
the WMU necessary for the proposed evaluation monitoring
program.

3. Any proposed additions or changes to the monitoring frequency,
sampling and analytical procedures or methods, or statistical
methods used at the WMU.

4. A detailed description of the measures to be taken to assess
the nature and extent of the release from the WMU.

OI'd                    LZHLSSF6OZ :ON XHd         HO1GO9/NTHOH 3OOSSHOSX3         LI:61 O3M Z6-?I-L00

WASTE DISCHARGE REQUIREMENTS
COUNTY OF FRESNO FOR CLOSURE OF
MENDOTA SOLID WASTE DISPOSAL SITE
CLASS III LANDFILL
FRESNO COUNTY

-9-

5.      A time schedule for completion of the evaluation monitoring
        program within 90 days.

e.  Within 180 days of finding a statistically significant increase,
    submit an engineering feasibility study for a corrective action
    program pursuant to Section 2550.10 of Chapter 15.

10. The Discharger shall properly maintain waste containment facilities and
    precipitation and drainage controls, and shall continue to monitor ground
    water, and surface waters in compliance with Monitoring and Reporting
    Program No. 91-225 throughout the post-closure maintenance period.

11. The post-closure maintenance period shall continue until the Board
    determines that wastes in the WMU no longer pose a threat to water
    quality.

12. The Discharger shall comply with the "Standard Provisions and Reporting
    Requirements for Waste Discharge Requirements", dated 1 March 1991, which
    are hereby incorporated into this Order.

13. The owner of the waste management facility shall have the continuing
    responsibility to assure protection of usable waters from discharged
    wastes and from gases and leachate generated by discharged wastes during
    the post-closure maintenance period of the WMU and during subsequent use
    of the property for other purposes.

COMPLIANCE SCHEDULE

14. The Discharger shall submit for approval by the Executive Officer,
    documentation as required for completion of the tasks outlined in these
    WDRs in accordance with the following time schedule:

    Task                                           Compliance Date

    a. CLOSURE                                     31 Dec 1991
       (see Discharge Specification B.9)

       Submit time schedule for
       closing the WMU.


II'd          L29L9SPH002 'ON XHd      HO3LOOG/NTHDIH YOSSH9SS3      69:8 OEM 2b-LI-LOO

WASTE DISCHARGE REQUIREMENTS
COUNTY OF FRESNO FOR CLOSURE OF
MENDOTA SOLID WASTE DISPOSAL SITE
CLASS III LANDFILL
FRESNO COUNTY

-10-

Task                                          Compliance Date

b. Detection Monitoring                       31 Dec 1991
   (see Provision C.8)

   Submit a time schedule for
   proposal of a Detection Monitoring
   Program.

c. Water Quality Protection Standard          31 Dec 1991
   (see Provision C.7)

   Submit a time schedule for proposal of
   a water quality protection standard for
   the WMU.

d. GROUND WATER LEVEL CONTROL                 31 Dec 1991

   Submit a time schedule for
   maintaining 5 foot separation between
   wastes and ground water.

e. BENCH MARKS                                28 Feb 1992

   Submit locations and elevations of
   bench marks.

f. FACILITY LIQUID MANAGEMENT                 31 Dec 1991

   Submit a work plan and time schedule
   for facility design, construction,
   and operation to protect the WMU from
   storm events, including precipitation
   and drainage controls required by
   Chapter 15.

g. LANDFILL GAS CONTROL                       31 Dec 1991

   Submit a time schedule for providing
   assurances that methane and other
   landfill gases will be adequately vented.


ZI'd        L2HL98F60Z 'ON XHd       HOZLOCHO/DNHQH 300SH9XSE       6S:E  OEM ZE-FI-I00

WASTE DISCHARGE REQUIREMENTS
COUNTY OF FRESNO FOR CLOSURE OF
MENDOTA SOLID WASTE DISPOSAL SITE
CLASS III LANDFILL
FRESNO COUNTY

-11-

Task                                          Compliance Date

h. WMU BOUNDARY                                31 Dec 1991

   Submit a copy of a recorded survey
   describing the boundary of the WMU,
   and a map delineating the WMU.


15. The Discharger shall comply with all applicable provisions of Chapter 15
    that are not specifically referred to in this Order.

16. The Board will review this Order periodically and will revise these
    requirements when necessary.


I, WILLIAM H. CROOKS, Executive Officer, do hereby certify that the foregoing is
a full, true, and correct copy of an Order adopted by the California Regional
Water Quality Control Board, Central Valley Region, on 22 November 1991.


                                    [signature]
                                    WILLIAM H. CROOKS, Executive Officer


CCC:fmc:11/22/91        AMENDED

embedding.qa_text: The fax transmission is from Chris Chalfant to John Wallace, with the company Fair Political Practices - State of CA. The fax number is 916-327-2026 and there are 13 pages. The content of the fax is as follows: John, Thank-you for the copy of the Chalmers letter you sent. I am faxing you a copy of the Waste Discharge Requirements for the Mendota Landfill and parts of the request for proposals issued by Fresno County. I would also like to note the following: I was not a designated employee of the Regional Water Quality Control Board. I did not, and was not, required to complete an annual statement of economic interest. Please call me if you need any more information. Thank-you Chris Chalfant III. SERVICES REQUIRED OF THE CONSULTANT Services of the consultant shall include, but not necessarily be limited to, the following: A. Review Disposal Site files for required information. B. Prepare timeline for project completion. C. Prepare draft closure studies (summarized under Tasks a., b., c., d., f., g., and h.; see Attachment "A") in accordance with Article 5, Chapter 15, Title 23, California Code of Regulations and Title 14, California Code of Regulations, and submit to Fresno County for its review and approval. As stated, Fresno County will prepare the documentation required under Task e. D. Prepare final closure studies and submit to the CRWQCB. Obtain approval of the required closure studies from the CRWQCB. E. Attend meetings with Fresno County staff and the CRWQCB. IV. SERVICES PROVIDED BY FRESNO COUNTY A. Provide access to Disposal Site files and assist with the location of Disposal Site information. B. Review and approve draft closure studies prior to submittal of final documents to the CRWQCB. C. Monitor progress of project to assure project deadlines are met. V. PROPOSAL SUBMITTAL REQUIREMENTS The submittal will be in two parts which are described in the following section: A. The first part will enable Fresno County to assess the general qualifications of the consultant. This will involve the consultant answering the following questions in the sequence listed below: 1. Firm name, address, and phone number. 2. Type of organization (individual, partnership, or corporation). 3. Firm principals who will be responsible for the project and their education, credentials, and experience. 4. Key personnel who will work on the project with their education, credentials, and experience on comparable projects. Provide organizational chart of firm. The County of Fresno, hereafter referred to as Discharger, owns an inactive Class III landfill on the south bank of the Fresno Slough, approximately 1.5 miles northeast of the City of Mendota in Section 29, T13S, R15E, MDB&M, as shown in Attachment A, which is incorporated herein as part of this Order.

Firm principals who will be responsible for the project and their education, credentials, and experience. 4. Key personnel who will work on the project with their education, credentials, and experience on comparable projects. Provide organizational chart of firm. Attachment "A" CALIFORNIA REGIONAL WATER QUALITY CONTROL BOARD CENTRAL VALLEY REGION ORDER NO. 91-225 WASTE DISCHARGE REQUIREMENTS FOR COUNTY OF FRESNO FOR CLOSURE OF MENDOTA SOLID WASTE DISPOSAL SITE CLASS III LANDFILL FRESNO COUNTY The California Regional Water Quality Control Board, Central Valley Region, (hereafter Board) finds that: 1. The County of Fresno, hereafter referred to as Discharger, owns an inactive Class III landfill on the south bank of the Fresno Slough, approximately 1.5 miles northeast of the City of Mendota in Section 29, T13S, R15E, MDB&M, as shown in Attachment A, which is incorporated herein as part of this Order.
